Name: Wabtec - Sri discovery call
Description: Use Case Description: Leveraging Machine Learning and Streaming/DLT, Wabtec Kinetic Inspection can further scale and optimize their machine vision ML workloads over their image data. The use case would involve data ingestion to stream large volumes of ima

Name: Wabtec - Sangeeta
Description: Sangeeta needs to consolidate and standardize data from many different ERP systems acquired by GE in the past for Wabtec. They specifically work with FP&A on analyzing payment history and want to build out some predictive capabilities as well as better integrate all of that data. Need to maintain data quality standards across functional areas while leveraging AI capabilities there.

Name: Wabtec - Trip Optimizer (Digital Intelligence Unit)
Description:
DI Unit works with their customers to provide software and digital technologies. They collect a large amount of data from each customer, mostly sensor data from the locomotives, that comes in a variety of types and delivery formats. 

They have internal teams with different requirements across apps team, data science and BI use cases. Also evaluating easier ways to enrich and monetize their data & AI assets.

Name: Wabtec - Financial Consolidation
Description: Brandon is looking at Databricks to unify and analyze massive supply chain, financial, and operational datasets in real time, making it possible to optimize inventory levels, detect anomalies, and generate accurate demand forecasts. This real-time visibility and machine learning-driven insight empower proactive management of risks and streamlined decision-making throughout the supply chain, reducing excess safety stock and improving responsiveness. This use case directly supports Wabtec’s 2025 objectives of driving growth through innovation, cost efficiency, and sustainability by enabling smarter, data-driven decision-making, better resource utilization, and faster responses to disruptions.

Name: Data Warehousing Opimization
Description: Mark's team is looking to use Databricks warehousing to unify rail data warehouse aggregates operational, maintenance, and customer data from rail systems, enabling real-time analytics and actionable business insights for improved service and decision-making. This facilitates predictive maintenance and dynamic resource allocation, transforming raw rail data into valuable information through centralized dashboards. This aligns directly with Wabtec’s 2025 goals to lead rail sector digitalization, enhance operational excellence, and drive sustainability by leveraging advanced analytics, AI-driven automation, and predictive maintenance to improve efficiency, reduce emissions, and maximize shareholder value.

Name: Wabtec - Wayside/Computer Vision
Description: Existing processes for collecting data is very manual and time consuming today. Their team spends a majority of their time on the pipelines, instead of building models. Which they want to flip.

Name: Wabtec - Life Cycle Cost Productivity
Description: Pradmaprabha is looking at using Databricks to unify data and apply predictive analytics, to be able to identify cost drivers, improve maintenance forecasting, and enhance asset performance. This initiative directly supports Wabtec’s 2025–2026 goals of boosting operational efficiency, reducing total cost of ownership, and driving sustainable, data-driven growth.

Name: Wabtec - Supply Chain
Description: The Digital Transformation team is looking at Databricks to unify Wabtec’s supply chain, logistics, and tariff data streams into a single Lakehouse platform, creating a near real-time dashboard for end-to-end visibility. This enables dynamic monitoring of supplier performance, tariff fluctuations, and shipment delays, allowing teams to mitigate disruptions and optimize global supply chain costs. This is directly in line with Doug's (CIO) goal for being able to implement AI within the next year.

Name: SQl Server Migration
Description: Wabtec is looking to migrate its legacy SQL Server data warehouses to the Databricks Lakehouse Platform to unify vast structured, unstructured, and streaming data from its digital intelligence systems like Condition Monitoring and Positive Train Control (PTC). This transformation will build a cloud-native, scalable foundation to accelerate advanced analytics, real-time reporting, and AI/ML model development for predictive maintenance and network optimization.

Name: Unified Industrial Data Intelligence Platform
Description:
Wabtec is looking to unify high-frequency IoT sensor streams with historical Oracle data to create a "digital thread" that provides near real-time, cross-plant visibility. This platform leverages Lakeflow Pipelines to process shop floor telemetry every 30 minutes, feeding predictive models that alert operators via PowerApps before defects exceed company averages.

This directly accelerating their 2026 Strategic Priorities of operational excellence and digital margin expansion through standardized quality and reduced maintenance overhead.

Name: DE for Mfg, Supply Chain & Defect Analytics
Description: Team imports data pulled from manufacturing systems, including testing equipment, and does analytic research (mostly human interactive), a small amount of data science/ML, and mostly data engineering to cleanse and structure data for downstream BI clients.  There are approx 2-3 analysts/engineers using Databricks for this use case.  The resulting BI data is consumed by about 300 users using a tool a homegrown reporting solution.  The reporting solution uses the rest-based APIS of Databricks jobs to get the data. The data received is from a variety of sources and formats including Snowflake, Azure Blob, and zip files.

Name: ML and Data Science (Manufacturing) (Kadie)
Description: Data science and ML workspace and ops platform for a ML skunk works team.  Team is mostly specialist "contractors" who work on a variety of projects for different teams across the manufacturing business unit.  Need to modernize and consolidate from a traditional ML-on-laptops scenarios.

Name: DE for high-speed photonics components Fab Lab tests
Description: Fabrication-level test results for HSPC are sent to Ciena by a 3rd party as CSVs from the test stations, These CSVs need to be processed to analyze the efficiency of the Fab tests. The data is cleansed and processed through Databricks. The serving layer is through the Ciena reporting portal JMP and direct CSV access too. test engineers/ and management use this information for capacity planning and overall monitoring of the testing performance. Snowflake is used as the final Gold ( or platinum) layer.

Name: DE for Mfg, Defect Analytics - Unstructured data (Images/Log etc)
Description:
Networking Equipment testing generates a lot of unstructured data in the form of images/logs etc which has to be processed for downstream applications. It is
 transferred through Kinesis to S3 cloud storage. AWS lambda is used for processing log files and building rules around what is processed and what is pending. These results are ingested into Databricks and served through Ciena reporting portal or direct through S3.

Name: ML-Wafer Level test result Prediction
Description: Develop mode to predict whether a wafer die will pass or fail wafer level tests given their results from fabrication level tests. Fab tests are performed first, and if we were able to bypass wafer level tests by predicting the results accurately, this could save Ciena a lot of time/money.

Name: DE/Delta/UC (Python/Kadie)-Variety of analytics using JMP, Excel, Matlab.
Description: DE/Delta/UC (Python/Kadie)- a variety of random analytics by various users using JMP, Excel, Matlab and other tools. Pipeline is running 24/7. Expect data volume to continue to grow, but now sure by how much or how fast.

Name: UC-Marketplace/Delta Sharing
Description: Ciena shares data with a few colleges and universities, which help them come up with optimization techniques and machine learning models. They want to evaluate ways to share the data in a more auditable manner.

Name: Migrate workloads from Snowflake
Description: 7/16/24 - Customer standardized on Snowflake for DW many years ago and today it's still their primary centralized data platform. So far they've shown zero interest in changing direction, so getting workloads from Snowflake to Databricks will be a tall order and will take a good amount of time. We continue to seek time with leadership to uncover pain and any cracks in the SF armor.

Name: GenAI - Foundation Model API experimentation
Description: An engineer did just enough to trigger an alert about Foundation Model API usage. We're still investigating.

Name: SQL: random DBSQL to do analysis on data (Kadie mostly)
Description: Use case is already in production; random SQL usage to do analysis on data. Most end users use PowerBI or SAS JMP (Jump), and DE use their own homegrown BI tool.

Name: Ciena GPT
Description: Ciena has one approved ChatGPT model served to a web application for their users. This use case is to train the model in DBX

Name: ETL Pipelines to SNOW
Description: We are aware of an Azure environment led by Rajesh (Dir. of IT) who is testing ETL pipelines to serve SNOW. We are unsure of the use case exactly at this point.

Name: Ciena: Reconciliation Pipeline
Description:
Implement Databricks Lakehouse to automate data reconciliation between 1,000+ tables across Oracle, SQL Server, Salesforce, and Snowflake, aligning with the "Optimize Service & Costs" objective in the IT/Manufacturing Outcome Map.

Objective: Build scalable reconciliation pipelines to identify data gaps, missing records, and mismatches between source systems and Snowflake, reducing manual effort by 70% and ensuring reliable downstream analytics.

Scope: Optimize ingestion workflows using Delta Live Tables and serverless compute to parallelize Spark jobs, resolving Azure network bottlenecks and reducing pipeline runtime by 50%.

BUSINESS OUTCOME:

Why Needed: Manual reconciliation of 1,000+ tables across fragmented systems (Oracle, Salesforce, Snowflake) causes $250K+ annual inefficiencies and risks inaccurate insights for critical business units.

Why Now: Ciena’s $180k Databricks commitment is underutilized, requiring immediate cost optimization and pipeline automation to justify ROI and support scaling to additional business units.

Measurable Value/Goal:

Reduce reconciliation errors by 80%, ensuring reliable data for downstream systems.

Cut pipeline costs by 40% via serverless compute and parallelization.

Achieve 90% automation of reconciliation workflows within 3 months.

Name: Serverless ML Training
Description:
Objective: Enable Ciena to build and train machine learning models using Databricks serverless compute for dynamic scaling, cost efficiency, and rapid experimentation.

Scope: Covers ML workloads from basic regression/classification to advanced time-series and deep learning; leverages Databricks serverless AutoML for forecasting, neural nets, and hyperparameter optimization.​

Data Volume: Models will be trained on large volumes of event logs, telemetry, and simulation data, requiring scalable GPU/CPU resources and secure data access.

Business Outcome:

Why Needed: Manual cluster management blocks agility and inflates cost; Ciena wants to empower engineering teams with instant, auto-scaled ML infrastructure.

Why Now: ML usage and data growth driving need to remove infrastructure bottlenecks and improve speed to insight.

Measurable Value/Goal: Faster model cycles, lower operational overhead, pay-per-use compute costs, improved ML productivity.

Long-term Vision: Serverless ML platform with seamless retraining, experiment tracking, and zero infrastructure management.

Name: Networking Analyzer Agent
Description:
Objective: Build an AI agent to analyze structured networking data, detect service degradation, and provide automated access auditing and performance insights across Ciena's hosted network services.​

Scope: Agent will query structured network telemetry data (KPIs, performance metrics, service logs) to identify degradation patterns over configurable time windows (last 7 days, 24 hours, real-time). Solution leverages Mosaic AI Agent Framework for customizable orchestration, model endpoints, and flexible control. Team evaluating three technical paths: AgentBricks (5-minute agent launch), Genie spaces (natural language querying), or full Mosaic AI Agent Framework implementation for maximum flexibility and visibility.​

Data Volume: Large-scale network telemetry from RAN, Transport, Core, Application Probes, and BSS/OSS interfaces. Ciena processes 40-90 million business events daily across sales, manufacturing, and operational systems. Network data stored in Delta Tables with Unity Catalog governance.​

Business Outcome:

Why Needed: Manual analysis of network performance degradation is slow and reactive. Operations teams need proactive, automated insights to detect issues before they impact customers. Current analytics lack natural language interface and flexible orchestration across multiple model providers.​

Why Now: Ciena has modernized data infrastructure with real-time streaming capabilities and cloud architecture. Team is exploring prompt optimization to enable single-prompt compatibility across all model providers, allowing users to select preferred LLMs while maintaining consistent agent behavior.​

Measurable Value/Goal: Faster incident detection and resolution, proactive network optimization, reduced mean-time-to-resolution (MTTR), improved service availability tracking, and enhanced customer experience through automated performance monitoring.​

Long-term Vision: Build comprehensive AI-powered network operations platform with closed-loop automation, real-time anomaly detection, predictive maintenance, and natural language access to network insights for operations teams.

Name: Dana Truck Fleet Sensors Phase 1
Description: Dana has sensors embedded on 100+ trucks sending telemetry data to IOT Hubs on Azure. Team streams this data into DLT and cuts files for various customer BI needs every 30 minutes. Plan on scaling to ~500 trucks in 2023. Then scaling up to >1000.

Name: CI/CD Use Case
Description:
USE CASE DESCRIPTION:

Customer is upgrading their CI/CD for application development, aiming to streamline code versioning and deployment on Azure.

Objective: Improve tracking and management of app changes through more robust data pipelines.

Scope: Focused on data science workloads, migrating workflows from AWS to Azure; data ingested with Snowflake, then moved to the Databricks Lakehouse.

Why Needed: Current support for app development is fragmented due to migration and tool changes.

Why Now: Recently completed AWS-to-Azure migration gives an opportunity to standardize processes and improve support.

Measurable Goal: Enable seamless version tracking and deployment for all app code changes; increase pipeline reliability.

Name: Machine Learning Use Cases 1-6
Description: We discussed with Bryan and Fatima their plans over the short term, and they are primarily focusing on moving somewhere between 15-18 pre-existing use cases onto Databricks. We do not currently know specific use cases, throughput, or other requirements, but will be conducting scoping and discovery to specifically scope these out. In addition, while we believe that this is primarily focused on migration of existing use cases, they had expressed interest in creating new use cases and models, which could be incorporated into these UCO to achieve proper sizing. This one represents models 1-6.

Name: Track and Trace - Agentic AI
Description:
ZR 10/13: In late September, we became aware of at least 2 agentic use cases that are in early stages of design and development. We have confirmed at least a Track and Trace use case, with a proposed architecture being provided to Zac Ryan on 10/10. There are several elements which seem to be appropriately centralized in Databricks, but we still need to discuss further elements which could be put into Databricks, and overall throughput in order to properly estimate $DBU. Since this use case seems to have potential customer facing implications (based on initial architectural document), and because it seems to run on a frequent, significantly more than daily cadence (up to several times an hour), achieving a higher level of consumption could be easier than other possible agentic use cases we have seen in the past. It incorporates agentic utilities, jobs, SQL-based queries and API calls, among several elements.

We hope to discuss this use case and others in our 10/22 on-prem meeting.

Milestones: Need to realign on go-live timing
Risks / Blockers: TBD
Link to Implementation Plan: No implementation plan at this time

Name: Observability + Monitoring
Description: Bryan had written that one of his goals were: "Expanding observability and monitoring beyond our data science platform to the broader data and analytics team, as well as possibly beyond to the IT org transaction systems (good to have stretch goals!)". This is a larger goal, and would likely have to incorporate data from other systems. It suggests governance, UC adoption, and expanding the platform to new users. This use case would go beyond just the UC enablement use case already entered, and include retraining of machine learning + AI models, and CICD processes to drive DBU.

Name: Penske Snowflake Profiler – Data Engineering & Pipeline Optimization
Description:
Objective:
Evaluate Penske’s existing Snowflake-based data engineering and pipelines using Databricks’ profiler capabilities to identify efficiency improvements that increase performance and reduce data processing/engineering costs.

Scope:
Work with Penske’s data and data science leadership to profile current Snowflake workloads (queries, pipelines, transformations), highlight bottlenecks and high-cost patterns, and propose targeted optimizations and/or candidate workloads for Databricks Lakehouse. This is explicitly not being positioned as a full Snowflake “takeout,” but as a cost/performance optimization and modernization exercise.

Data Volume/Types:
Existing Snowflake data warehouses, ETL/ELT pipelines, and analytic workloads in production; details to be refined during intro/scoping with Vishwa and Tony.

Business Outcome:

Why Needed: Current Snowflake pipelines may be over-consuming resources, under-optimized, or overly complex, leading to higher spend and slower data delivery to downstream analytics and data science teams.

Why Now: Vishwa and Tony are interested in concrete, data-driven insight into where they can gain efficiency and performance without committing to an immediate platform displacement.

Measurable Value/Goal: Reduce data processing cost and runtime for key workflows; simplify pipeline architecture; build a fact base for future platform strategy decisions.

Long-term Vision: A more efficient, observable data engineering estate, with clear understanding of when/where Databricks Lakehouse adds value alongside or instead of Snowflake.

Name: Timken: Adam Hopkins LLM's + DBX AWS Trial
Timken: Unity Catalog Migration
Description: Timken seeks to modernize its data architecture by implementing a data lake with Gold/Silver/Bronze layers, enabling efficient data processing and transformation. Integration with Purview will provide improved data lineage and governance, addressing limitations in tracking and managing data transformations. Unity Catalog will enhance data visibility and management, ensuring compliance and enabling seamless collaboration across teams. The potential migration from Synapse to Databricks SQL will improve performance and reduce costs, while supporting scalable analytics workflows for self-service capabilities.

Name: Timken: Jacob Kerber - Gen AI Use Cases for R&D Team
Description: Timken seeks to deploy Databricks Unity Catalog to streamline data governance and enhance data management capabilities. The organization is exploring advanced AI/Gen AI use cases such as automating insights, developing LLM models, and enabling seamless integration with current tools. A phased approach, starting with POCs in Q1 2025, will validate the platform’s impact on operational efficiency, collaboration, and cost-effectiveness.

Name: Timken - Predictive Pricing
Description: Develop a predictive pricing model that integrates historical sales data, production costs, and market trends to suggest optimal prices for bearings products.

Name: Agent Framework and Eval
Description: Jacob Kerber from Timken has expressed interest in building high quality agentic AI systems and wants to learn more about the features and capabilities databricks has to offer. We scheduled a meeting with him on the 30th to discuss. We will build out more of this use case with rewquirements after we do the demo and perform more discovery

Name: TimkenGPT Phase 1
Description:
Jacob Kerber has already developed a robust TimkenAI application that leverages Azure AI Search and Langfuse to vectorize unstructured data and documents across Timken, with Azure OpenAI serving as the large language model at its core. This solution has enabled efficient data processing and retrieval throughout the organization.

However, to further elevate the accuracy and effectiveness of data retrieval, the team is now pursuing a transformative upgrade. The goal is to completely reimagine and rebuild the end-to-end TimkenGPT platform on Databricks. This next-generation approach will:

Consolidate all unstructured data into a unified Vector Search architecture.

Utilize Agent Bricks KBQA for advanced knowledge-based question answering.

Deliver a seamless, one-stop shop for Timken intelligence, empowering internal employees with faster, more precise, and comprehensive insights.

Name: Supply Chain Tariff Analysis
Description: Organizations in global manufacturing face mounting complexity and increased costs due to rapidly evolving tariffs, disparate data sources, and fragmented supply chain visibility. Leadership teams lack a consolidated view of order, sales, and shipment flows, making it difficult to assess the true impact of tariff changes and respond quickly to shifting trade policies. Without consolidated analytics, cost pressures rise, errors proliferate, and opportunities to optimize sourcing or re-route supply chains are often missed. As a result, businesses are seeking scalable, reusable analytics platforms that can integrate data from systems like SAP, SQL Server, and Databricks. This enables proactive scenario modeling, real-time cost tracking, and informed executive decision-making in an unpredictable tariff environment

Name: Heat Trend Prediction
Description: Steel manufacturers struggle to leverage disconnected machine data, manual CSV processes, and limited model deployment to prevent downtime and improve product consistency. With 900 hours of annual furnace downtime recently uncovered—translating to substantial lost revenue—current analysis is insufficiently actionable, and real-time insights remain largely out of reach. The lack of scalable machine connectivity and advanced data science restricts scenario planning and root-cause analysis, hindering quality improvement and cost savings. There is an urgent need for a robust platform that consolidates live data from Heat Tree furnaces, supports continuous model updates, and enables predictive maintenance and process optimization. Successfully implementing this use case in Databricks could unlock millions in savings, deliver rapid value to operational teams like Praveen and Bernie, and serve as a catalyst for broader digital transformation across the enterprise

Name: Timken + Argano - Agentic AI and MCP Server
Description: Timken is current Argano SAP customer, also using Databricks. In discussions around Agentic AI and MCP Server optimization.

Name: Enterprise SAP-to-Lakehouse Integration
Description:
Current State: Approximately 80% of Timken's operational data sits in a single instance of SAP (ECC/HANA). This data is currently siloed and difficult for data scientists and analysts to access for cross-functional projects.

The Challenge: High-priority, funded AI projects are currently blocked because the data remains trapped in SAP. The team needs a "fire alarm" solution to move this data into the cloud lakehouse.

Databricks Solution: Implementing a high-speed ingestion layer (potentially via Fivetran or a specialized partner like Sellable Technologies) to land SAP data into Azure-based Databricks. This would involve a transition strategy from their current ECC version toward a future-state SAP Datasphere/BDC (Business Data Cloud) integration using Delta Sharing.

Business Value:

Data Democratization: Breaks down the walls between SAP and the rest of the business, enabling the "AI Innovation Team" to apply ML to 80% of the company's operational data.

Speed to Insight: Moves the company away from batch processing toward a more real-time, unified view of the enterprise.

Name: Supply Chain "Timken AI" & Agentic Framework
Description:
Current State: Timken has developed an internal Large Language Model (LLM) called "Timken AI." The team is currently building custom MCP (Model Context Protocol) servers to help the AI interact with company data.

The Challenge: The team is in the "infancy" stage and needs to decide between building custom infrastructure or leveraging commercial tools within Databricks to host agents and retrieve information.

Databricks Solution: Utilizing Databricks Mosaic AI Agent Framework. This includes hosting Mosaic AI Model Serving for their LLM and using Vector Search to enable RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation). Julia is specifically exploring Databricks "Agents"—apps that can execute tools and retrieve supply chain data autonomously.

Business Value:

Efficiency: Allows supply chain users to query complex data using natural language.

Maintenance: Reduces the "technical debt" of building and maintaining custom-coded MCP servers by using Databricks' native, managed environment.

Name: HR Data Pipeline Migration & Dashboard Optimization
Description:
Current State: A "Frankensteined" data pipeline that has been pieced together over several years. The current architecture struggles with high data volumes, leading to slow-loading Power BI dashboards and frequent technical issues.

The Challenge: The existing system is reactive and lacks the scalability to support advanced analytics or predictive modeling.

Databricks Solution: Transitioning from legacy scripts to a structured Medallion Architecture. This involves using Delta Live Tables (DLT) for orchestration and automated data engineering to streamline the flow from raw HR data to "Gold" level tables.

Business Value: * Performance: Significantly faster Power BI reporting for HR leadership.

Scalability: Ability to handle larger historical datasets without performance degradation.

Future-Proofing: Establishes the clean data foundation required to launch Machine Learning workflows (e.g., attrition prediction or talent acquisition analytics).

Name: SAP Ingestion for Analytics and Reporting
Description: Dart is primarily an SAP shop and uses SAP across multiple different business domains. They are using Azure Data Factory to write data feeds to Databricks. Current process is primarily truncate and replace. They do basic transformations on the data and serve it in a gold layer for PowerBI reporting for Supply Chain, Finance, and Sales. PBI is hooked up to Databricks via Warehouse Endpoint.

Name: Production KPI - Schedule Attainment
Description: This KPI tracks how often the production team meets the target level of production and provides an important way to set performance benchmarks, fine-tune work order delivery time estimates, and make sure that performance issues aren't causing costly delays.

Name: Unity Catalog Migration
Description: Dart currently uses Hive Metastore. As part of Databricks recommended platform best practices, we suggested Dart consider moving to Unity Catalog to adopt new Advanced Analytics/GenAI/Data Science features that are coming + give them a way to manage groups/data security.

Name: Pricing Elasticity
Description: Price elasticity analysis will allow Dart to identify price-sensitive customer segments and products by looking at competitor pricing. By segmenting customers based on their price sensitivity, Dart can tailor pricing strategies and promotions to maximize revenue from each segment.

Name: Recommendation Engine for RFP
Description: Dart wants to build an application on Databricks leveraging SAP data and external competitive pricing data to provide accurate and competitive pricing for RFPs. 4-10 users will utilize an application, input SKU data, and get an output that would recommend pricing or give a pricing band based on customer data + modeled pricing data.

Name: Air Products - Enterprise Data Warehouse with Change Data Capture Architecture
Description: Currently missing SLAs related to SAP ECC QlikReplicate CDC jobs.  In early stages of migrating to RedShift (leverage S3 as staging area), but it's not scaling to meet their needs and they have released a Data Warehouse RFP to explore alternatives.

Name: Data Governance - Security and Access Control
Description: Standing up a data lake lake leveraging AWS native tools, but there are gaps in user access and security.  Need a simplified way to govern data with row level security and dynamic data masking capabilities.

Name: Unified Data Engineering & Data Science Architecture
Description: Currently have too many tools with too many UIs for user access (QlikSense, SageMaker, Alation, etc.) and ideally looking to have a simplified user experience to support multiple personas. A short-term approach would be to use Databricks as ETL/DW. ML would be a long-term goal.

Name: Air Products UC
Description: 7/07 - Currently in month 4 of POC. ~3 weeks left remaining. DR/Qlik/AI use cases remaining. Daily calls with customer to move POC forward. Customer evaluated Snowflake in November 2024.

Name: Air Products AI/ML
Description: 8/6 Air Products awarded Databricks as the Redshift migration platform. In parallel, AIr Products is exploring several AI/ML use cases to leverage Databricks.

Name: Analog Devices - Anomaly Detection
Description:
Overview of project:

Scope
Pilot on STDF parametric data from Final Test and Sort for selected high-priority product line(s)  (e.g., AD5562).
Data ingested from Central File Collector (CFC), normalized in Delta Lake.
Contextual meta-data (lot, wafer, fab, program, site) stored separately from parametric data, with referential integrity enforced.  Enables normalized joins and consistent analytics.
Anomaly models focused on detecting statistical outliers and distribution shifts across key test parameters.
MVP includes model training, prediction, and visualization for selected product line(s).
Timeline
~ 8 weeks including ingestion, meta-data mapping, ML modeling, and baseline against existing solutions.
Timeline dependent on engagement from Databricks team & stakeholders
Strategic Fit
Foundational capability that integrates AI/ML, compute, data governance in single platform
Meta-data normalization solves one of the biggest blockers in yield analytics, enabling reusability, governance, and cross-domain integration (SPC, ML, traceability).
Demonstrates scalable, ML-ready foundation for detecting excursions and correlating across sites and test types.
Reduces fragmentation of analytics between EMA, AKS/Snowflake pipelines, and PowerBI mashups.
Supports enterprise-wide shift toward AI/ML-driven yield improvement

Name: Gentherm- Migrate Finance Workload from PowerBI into Silver/Gold layers
Description:
Gentherm is currently exposes (via databricks) entire bronze layer for Finance Actuals and Budgets (from SAP) as well as multiple Fact and Dimension tables in PowerBI.  Mass datasets (non-aggregated) are synced overnight to PowerBI- where all the joins (lots of many:1) occur, as well as around ~20 DAX expressions (calculated fields) occur.

Business users access these datasets in PowerBI- but no visualization is occuring.  Finance users export directly to excel.

Account team (Pete, Rick) met with IT leader Sharad Singhai and reviewed data model and logic in PowerBI.  Recommendation is to start pulling fact and dimension joins as well as dax logic into Databricks.  Long term finance users should be accessing databricks directly- however short term would be migrate these workloads but still follow the DB-PBI-excel pattern to support business.

Name: Gentherm - SAP Data Migration
Description:
Working with Sharad on initial discovery- essentially Sharad wants to ingest and govern all SAP data into Databricks.  Ultimate goal is to get down to full data lineage  - source system, which catalogs do they live in and what is the consumption across all business systems (including SAP, Infor (SQL), Dynamics, Navision into Central Data Lake.

2 instances of ECC Patch 8 apply.  Currently leveraging Kagool Velocity as ingestion tool, CDC which brings display names and german table names.  Would ideally like to use Databricks directly.

DWH, ML, Data science workloads apply.

Name: Gentherm HCM Data Ingestion
Description:
Use Case: Gentherm needs to centralize and protect HR datasets containing PII within Databricks, applying strict governance as data moves across enterprise finance and HR systems.

Objective: Enable secure streaming and masking of personnel data, ensuring only appropriate users can view confidential fields.

Scope: Applies to all HR data flowing from Gentherm’s cloud systems into the Databricks lakehouse, covering data privacy for both HR and finance teams.

Data Volume: Involves all current and future HR records, with scalable architecture to handle data growth.

Business Outcome:

Why Needed: Protect employee privacy, meet compliance standards, and reduce risk of unauthorized access.

Why Now: Gentherm is modernizing cloud data operations and has urgent business requests to close gaps in HR data security.

Measurable Goal: Automate policy tagging and masking for all HR records, with zero unauthorized access incidents.

Name: Gentherm - Scrap Reduction Insights
Description:
Gentherm's number one issue up to the exec level is scrap. The cost associated with scrap as well as indirect costs such as premium freight are a big discussion point. There is not currently a centralized mechanism to view and analyze scrap and RCA.

1) Ensure pipeline is sufficient for structured plant/part level scrap data for daily ingestion
2) Ingest quality 8D root cause reports, extract information, summarize and categorize RCA's on the part number level
3) Build dashboards and chat bot to interrogate scrap data.  
4) Later phases may contain NBA engine, scrap forecasting and actionable insights based on scrap categories.

Decision timeline is March, 2024 with migration starting in July, 2024. Data sources primarily 15 - 20 ERP systems and 6+ CRM systems migrating to Azure cloud leveraging Databricks Intelligence Platform for Data and AI use cases. Migration will start in July, 2024 and project is funded.

Name: POM Implementation
Description: Kyle to describe

Name: Hillenbrand: Performance Operations Management (POM)
Description:
Use Case

Modernize data infrastructure by migrating legacy systems to Databricks Lakehouse architecture, enabling advanced analytics and AI/BI use cases across supply chain, finance, and CRM reporting. This aligns with the "Improve Industrial Productivity" objective in the Manufacturing Outcome Map.
Develop Performance Operations Management (POM) AI/BI Layer with MARLABS to transform business operations using Genie for natural language queries and dashboards built on top of the lakehouse architecture. This initiative will prioritize use cases based on business value and ROI.
Support future initiatives such as Supply Chain Inventory Ops (SIOP), Finance Data Lake, and Spend Cube, creating scalable solutions for operational efficiency and cost savings.
BUSINESS OUTCOME
Why Needed: Hillenbrand struggles with scaling efficiently and retrofitting legacy systems, leading to inefficiencies in data accessibility and operational workflows. Their current architecture limits the ability to derive actionable insights from advanced analytics.
Why Now: Recent advancements in AI/BI technologies present an opportunity to modernize their data infrastructure while reducing costs associated with legacy systems. The organization is actively prioritizing initiatives that deliver measurable ROI and align with their strategic goals.
Measurable Value/Goal: Implementation will reduce operational costs by 30%, improve data accessibility for decision-making by 40%, and enable faster insights generation through AI/BI use cases.

Name: Hillenbrand AI Readiness
Description:
Hillenbrand is in the midst of a multi-year databricks rollout.  The first year has focused on finance and CRM/ERP migrations from legacy on-prem warehouses.

In FY2026 they are embarking on their AI journey focusing on Databricks Capabilities.

Name: Milacron BI Migration – Hillenbrand Separation
Description:
USE CASE DESCRIPTION
Objective: Support Milacron’s migration of Business Intelligence and reporting capabilities as Hillenbrand completes divestiture.

Scope: Migration involves BI workloads, data quality frameworks, and governance models previously tied to Hillenbrand. Primary users are BI analysts, program managers, and business leaders at Milacron.

Business Outcome – Why Needed

Separation requires Milacron to establish independent BI tools and governance to support ongoing operations.

Independence ensures Milacron can maintain control of KPIs, data quality, and reporting cadence without Hillenbrand dependencies.

Strategic urgency: Must stand up parallel BI infrastructure now to avoid disruptions in business reporting during divestiture.

Measurable: Success will be defined by continuity of reporting and governance at go-live (no interruption to BI processes).

Name: Hillenbrand Foundational Stabilization
Description: The intent of this use case is to support ongoing foundational stabilization for Hillenbrand coming out of an architectural advisory review.

Name: Penske Truck Leasing: Data as One Stop Shop - Lakehouse
Description: PTL Data Science team struggling with disparate, silo'd data. Want to explore DBX as a "Data as a One Stop Shop" lakehouse option. Being pressured to use Snowflake, but have not pursued. PTL Is large AWS shop, so we'll need to fit within AWS architecture to serve as Lakehouse.
